items.headline: Stadium neighborhood subcommittee meets to discuss Levi's Stadium relations
items.summary: Santa Clara formed an ad hoc subcommittee focused on Stadium Neighborhood Relations. This suggests ongoing discussions about how the stadium affects nearby residents, including potential concerns about traffic, noise, or development impacts.

items.headline: City launching business license amnesty program for local shops
items.summary: Cupertino is rolling out an amnesty program to help shopping centers and small businesses get compliant with business licensing requirements, plus launching a "Shop Local" campaign to support retail and startups. This could affect local business owners who may have fallen behind on licenses.

items.headline: New mixed-use residential development approved for East Middlefield Road
items.summary: Mountain View approved a mixed-use residential development at 490 East Middlefield Road on the second reading of a development agreement. This project will add housing and likely commercial space to that location.

items.headline: Mountain View applies for state housing incentive funding
items.summary: The city submitted an application for Round 4 of California's Prohousing Incentive Program, which provides state funding to cities that adopt policies to increase housing production.

items.headline: City seeking public input on how to spend federal housing grant money
items.summary: Santa Clara is accepting comments on the draft plan for spending HUD federal housing grants for fiscal year 2026-27. This is a chance for residents to weigh in on affordable housing priorities and initiatives.

items.headline: City holding community meeting on safe parking program
items.summary: Sunnyvale is hosting a public meeting on Monday, March 23 to discuss a safe parking program. This appears to be a new or expanded initiative to address parking or vehicle-related issues in the city.
